execution_environment.docker_command: docker run --rm --entrypoint bash -v $(pwd):/workspace --network host vllm/vllm-openai:latest -c "pip3 install pytest -q 2>&1 | tail -1 && cd /workspace/artifacts/17-triton-rmsnorm && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v 2>&1"

test_cases.name: test_rms_norm_correctness
test_cases.description: Triton RMSNorm matches PyTorch reference for various (N,D) shapes

test_cases.name: test_rms_norm_dtypes
test_cases.description: RMSNorm works with fp16 and fp32 inputs

test_cases.name: test_rms_norm_unit_variance
test_cases.description: With weight=ones, output RMS should be ~1.0

test_cases.name: test_fused_add_rmsnorm_identity_residual
test_cases.description: With zero residual, fused RMSNorm equals regular RMSNorm

test_cases.name: test_fused_add_rmsnorm_residual_preserved
test_cases.description: Returned residual equals x + residual (pre-norm sum, matches vLLM convention)

test_cases.name: test_various_hidden_sizes
test_cases.description: RMSNorm handles hidden sizes both below and above BLOCK_SIZE=1024

test_cases.name: test_determinism
test_cases.description: Same input produces identical output across 5 runs

test_cases.name: test_compatible_with_triton_primer
test_cases.description: RMSNorm follows Triton Primer patterns, no NaN/Inf

test_cases.name: test_residual_pattern_for_llama_layer
test_cases.description: Fused RMSNorm supports LlamaDecoderLayer residual pattern

test_cases.name: test_teaching_example_traceable
test_cases.description: D=8 small example that readers can verify by hand

test_cases.name: test_teaching_numerical_stability
test_cases.description: fp32 accumulation prevents overflow with large fp16 values

test_cases.name: test_shape_mismatch_raises
test_cases.description: Weight shape mismatch with input raises AssertionError

notes: Tests are written and syntax-verified. GPU (CUDA) execution required — run in vLLM Docker container. All tests use @needs_cuda and @needs_triton decorators to safely skip when dependencies are missing.
